pattern: A greedy left-to-right scan that closes each segment the moment it first becomes new, backed by a set of already-emitted segments, runs in linear total work.
hints: You never look ahead: you extend the current run one character at a time and stop as soon as the run is something you have not produced before.
Keep a set of the segments you have already emitted. The current run grows until it is absent from that set.
Walk an index, accumulate characters into a buffer, and whenever the buffer is not in the seen-set, push it to the answer, mark it seen, and reset the buffer.

edge_inputs.description: all identical characters force growing prefixes
mixed run from the worked example
all distinct letters each become their own segment
two-letter blocks where a one-char segment can repeat before its longer form appears
long single-letter run yields increasingly long segments

stress.description: 5k random lowercase letters - exercises set lookups on growing buffers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alpha = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: small alphabet maximizes collisions and forces longer segments

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def partitionString(self, s):
        res = []
        seen = set()
        start = 0
        i = 0
        n = len(s)
        while i < n:
            seg = s[start:i + 1]
            if seg not in seen:
                res.append(seg)
                seen.add(seg)
                start = i + 1
            i += 1
        return res
